plan_summary.plan_name: Paperclip Automation Pilot Line (Builder Pragmatic Integration)
plan_summary.primary_goal: Build a fully automated, end-to-end pilot paperclip line in 4,000 sq ft of the Cleveland building that takes API orders and stages labeled parcels for UPS/FedEx pickup with ≤2 hours/week of manual intervention, on a $300–500K budget under the Builder strategy.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests the manual-intervention-hours margin against the 2 hr/week ceiling; the budget surplus against bottom-up program spend; the OPC UA middleware bid margin against the $75K custom-IO-fallback trigger; the API p99 latency margin against the 100 ms HMI-pivot trigger; and the on-site PLC-expert contract margin against the $25K Phase-2 stabilization floor. Cleveland permits, used-wire-bender modern fieldbus availability, and UPS/FedEx fixed-table staging are treated as unmodelled existential gates.

key_values.label: Program budget ceiling
key_values.comment: Upper bound of the $300–500K Builder envelope; absorbs hardware (~50%), OPC UA middleware ($50K), on-site PLC tuning ($15K), and 10% contingency reserves.
key_values.source_text: Total project budget range maximum: $500,000 — input to financial model.

key_values.label: Manual-intervention ceiling for feasibility-proof
key_values.comment: Above 2 hr/week the end-to-end demonstration fails the feasibility metric; the primary success criterion of the Builder strategy.
key_values.source_text: If documented manual intervention time exceeds 120 minutes across a standard operational week, then the end-to-end demo fails feasibility proof.

key_values.label: OPC UA middleware bid threshold for custom-IO pivot
key_values.comment: Premortem trigger: if the lowest qualified bid exceeds $75K the project reverts to the high-risk custom I/O translator path, breaking the Decision-3 abstraction-layer thesis.
key_values.source_text: If lowest qualified OPC UA middleware bid exceeds $75,000, project reverts to custom I/O translator model.

key_values.label: API job-queue p99 latency HMI-pivot trigger
key_values.comment: Premortem trigger: if 99th-percentile API job-queue latency exceeds 100 ms under stress test, the project pivots from API-only to HMI-controlled demonstration.
key_values.source_text: If the 99th percentile API job queue latency exceeds 100ms during stress testing, then the project pivots to HMI-controlled demonstration.

key_values.label: On-site PLC-expert contract cap for Phase-2 stabilization
key_values.comment: Review-plan gate: the mandatory 1-week on-site PLC integration contract must clear under $25K within 21 days; above this breaches the Issue-3 ring-fenced reserve.
key_values.source_text: Within 21 days, secure signed contract guaranteeing 1 week on-site PLC integration support, cost less than $25,000.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Projected manual-intervention hours per week under Phase-6 demo conditions
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives whether the 2 hr/week feasibility-proof threshold clears; Issue 2 names a 10% material-variance scenario that pushes manual handling well above the ceiling.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Bottom-up: exception rate × time-per-exception × shift hours; sensitivity to Risk 2 wire-bender PLC instability and Risk 5 grading-protocol variance.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Bottom-up total program cost across hardware, middleware, expert, and contingency
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Tests whether the $500K ceiling holds; Risk 3 names a $20–50K overrun, and Issue 1 adds a 15% spare-parts buffer on top of the hardware CAPEX.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Sum machinery ($150–250K) + OPC UA middleware + on-site expert + edge compute + label/conveyor + permits + 10% contingency + 15% spare-parts buffer; sensitivity to Risks 1, 2, 3.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Lowest-qualified OPC UA middleware bid received
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Above $75K the project reverts to the custom-IO translator path per the premortem trigger, which the Builder strategy specifically exists to avoid.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: RFP to 3+ controls-integration specialists; benchmark against industry-standard OPC UA gateway+abstraction-layer engagements; sensitivity to wire-bender protocol complexity.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved API job-queue p99 latency under stress test
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Above 100 ms the project pivots to HMI-controlled demonstration per the premortem trigger; this invalidates the API-only feasibility claim.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Edge-compute hardware spec × OPC UA round-trip × queue backpressure modelling; benchmark against industry MQTT/REST control-loop latencies in similar pilots.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Achieved on-site PLC-expert contract cost for the 1-week stabilization window
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Above $25K the contract breaches the review-plan Phase-2 stabilization budget and forces reallocation from spare-parts or contingency reserves.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Solicit 2–3 quotes from specialized PLC integration contractors; sensitivity to mobilization-time premium and wire-bender protocol obscurity.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Manual-intervention margin against the 2 hr/week feasibility ceiling
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin clears the feasibility-proof success metric; negative margin invalidates the central Builder thesis regardless of how clean the integration is otherwise.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Program budget surplus against the $500K ceiling
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive surplus preserves the Risk-3 contingency and Issue-1 spare-parts reserve; negative surplus forces scope cuts or sponsor capital call.

recommended_first_calculations.label: OPC UA middleware bid margin against the $75K custom-IO trigger
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin keeps the Decision-3 abstraction-layer strategy intact; negative margin forces the custom-IO translator pivot and scope creep onto the internal developer.

recommended_first_calculations.label: API p99 latency margin against the 100 ms HMI-pivot trigger
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin preserves the API-only Phase-6 demonstration target; negative margin forces HMI-controlled fallback and invalidates the autonomous-flow feasibility claim.

recommended_first_calculations.label: On-site PLC-expert contract margin against the $25K Phase-2 cap
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Positive margin secures the Issue-3 mandatory stabilization without reallocating reserves; negative margin reaches into the spare-parts or contingency pool and weakens later-phase resilience.

unmodelled_gates.label: Building, electrical, and OSHA permits secured before Phase 2 rigging
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Risk 1 names permitting as the primary regulatory blocker; without permits before Phase 2 the rigging crew cannot install machinery and the schedule slips 4–8 weeks regardless of integration progress.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: 4–8 week schedule delay; $10–20K additional cost; possible stop-work order during rigging; entire 6-phase sequence cascades.

unmodelled_gates.label: A used wire bender within $20–40K supports a modern documented fieldbus (Modbus TCP / Ethernet/IP / Profinet)
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: The Decision-3 OPC UA abstraction layer presupposes a modern fieldbus on the used bender; without it the integration reverts to custom I/O translation and the Builder strategy thesis collapses regardless of budget.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Custom I/O translator path activated; $15–30K extra cost per Risk 2; 4–6 weeks development time; internal-developer scope creeps off API into low-level driver work.

unmodelled_gates.label: UPS/FedEx accept fixed-table parcel staging without dynamic conveyor integration
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Premortem trigger: if carriers require dynamic conveyor staging beyond $40K mechanical redesign, the project pivots to B2B-volume-only shipment and the consumer-API feasibility claim is invalidated.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: $40K+ mechanical redesign required or pivot to B2B-only shipment; Phase 5/6 demonstration scope shifts; carrier-API integration narrative weakens.
